animations.alt_text: Four Byrne colour swatches sit beside sample lines and an angle mark on a cream page.
animations.description: Reference scene showing the Byrne palette, stroke weights, and angle marks.
animations.title: Byrne palette card

animations.alt_text: A point appears, then a line, then a curved and a straight line are contrasted.
animations.description: Introduces the Euclidean ideas of a point, a line, and a straight line.

nc_references.statement: Identify horizontal and vertical lines and pairs of perpendicular and parallel lines
Describe, sketch and draw using conventional terms and notation

animations.title: Definitions I, II, IV — point, line, straight line

animations.alt_text: One arm rotates around a vertex so the angle changes from right to obtuse to acute.
animations.description: Shows how a right angle relates to obtuse and acute angles.

nc_references.statement: Recognise angles as a property of shape or a description of a turn
Identify right angles
Identify acute and obtuse angles
Describe, sketch and draw using conventional terms and notation

animations.title: Definitions X–XII — right, obtuse, and acute angles

animations.alt_text: A point and radius create a circle, then a diameter and coloured semicircle are added.
animations.description: Builds a circle from a centre and radius, then highlights the diameter and semicircle.

nc_references.statement: Illustrate and name parts of circles, including radius, diameter and circumference
Derive and illustrate properties of circles and other plane figures

animations.title: Definitions XV–XVIII — circle, centre, diameter, semicircle

animations.alt_text: Three triangles appear side by side, each labelled by its side properties.
animations.description: Compares equilateral, isosceles, and scalene triangles.

nc_references.statement: Compare and classify geometric shapes, including triangles, based on their properties and sizes
Derive and illustrate properties of triangles and other plane figures

animations.title: Definitions XXIV–XXVI — triangles by side

animations.alt_text: Three triangles appear with angle marks showing right, obtuse, and acute classifications.
animations.description: Compares right-angled, obtuse-angled, and acute-angled triangles.

nc_references.statement: Compare and classify geometric shapes, including triangles, based on their properties and sizes
Derive and illustrate properties of triangles and other plane figures

animations.title: Definitions XXVII–XXIX — triangles by angle

animations.alt_text: A square morphs through several quadrilateral families while their defining properties are highlighted.
animations.description: Compares square, rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram, and trapezium families.

nc_references.statement: Compare and classify geometric shapes, including quadrilaterals and triangles, based on their properties and sizes
Derive and illustrate properties of quadrilaterals and other plane figures

animations.title: Definitions XXX–XXXIV — quadrilaterals

animations.alt_text: Two lines extend in both directions while spacing markers show they remain parallel.
animations.description: Shows that parallel lines stay equidistant and never meet.

nc_references.statement: Identify horizontal and vertical lines and pairs of perpendicular and parallel lines
Describe, sketch and draw using conventional terms and notation

animations.title: Definition XXXV — parallel lines

animations.alt_text: Two points appear and a straight line is drawn cleanly between them.
animations.description: Establishes the basic act of joining two points with a straight line.

nc_references.statement: Derive and use the standard ruler and compass constructions

animations.title: Postulate I — draw a straight line between two points

animations.alt_text: A short segment grows in both directions to become a longer straight line.
animations.description: Shows a line segment being produced beyond its endpoints.

nc_references.statement: Derive and use the standard ruler and compass constructions

animations.title: Postulate II — extend a finite straight line

animations.alt_text: A point and a fixed radius produce a full circle through a compass-style sweep.
animations.description: Shows a circle swept from a chosen centre and radius.

nc_references.statement: Illustrate and name parts of circles, including radius, diameter and circumference
Derive and use the standard ruler and compass constructions

animations.title: Postulate III — describe a circle with any centre and distance

animations.alt_text: Two circles cross above a base line to create an equilateral triangle.
animations.description: Builds an equilateral triangle from a single given segment using two circles.

nc_references.statement: Draw 2D shapes using given dimensions and angles
Derive and use the standard ruler and compass constructions

animations.title: Proposition I — construct an equilateral triangle on a given line

animations.alt_text: Several auxiliary lines and circles show how a given length is copied from a new point.
animations.description: Constructs a new segment from a point equal in length to a given segment.

nc_references.statement: Derive and use the standard ruler and compass constructions

animations.title: Proposition II — copy a line segment from a given point

animations.alt_text: A longer line is marked by a circle so a shorter equal segment is cut off from it.
animations.description: Cuts a line so that part of it matches a shorter given segment.

nc_references.statement: Derive and use the standard ruler and compass constructions

animations.title: Proposition III — cut off a segment equal to a shorter one

animations.alt_text: Compass arcs and a final line split one angle cleanly into two equal parts.
animations.description: Bisects a given angle using equal marks and an equilateral triangle construction.

nc_references.statement: Derive and use the standard ruler and compass constructions

animations.title: Proposition IX — bisect a rectilinear angle

animations.alt_text: A line segment gains auxiliary circles and a final mark at its midpoint.
animations.description: Locates the midpoint of a line segment by construction.

nc_references.statement: Derive and use the standard ruler and compass constructions

animations.title: Proposition X — bisect a finite straight line

animations.alt_text: An equilateral-triangle construction creates a vertical line from a point on a base line.
animations.description: Constructs a line perpendicular to a given line at a point on it.

nc_references.statement: Identify horizontal and vertical lines and pairs of perpendicular and parallel lines
Derive and use the standard ruler and compass constructions

animations.title: Proposition XI — draw a perpendicular from a point on a line

animations.alt_text: A point above a line uses a circle and midpoint construction to create a perpendicular.
animations.description: Drops a perpendicular from a point outside a line down to the line.

nc_references.statement: Derive and use the standard ruler and compass constructions

animations.title: Proposition XII — draw a perpendicular from an external point

animations.alt_text: Two adjacent coloured angles sit on a straight line and are shown to combine to 180 degrees.
animations.description: Demonstrates that adjacent angles on a straight line sum to two right angles.

nc_references.statement: Identify angles at a point on a straight line and 1/2 a turn
Apply the properties of angles at a point and angles at a point on a straight line

animations.title: Proposition XIII — angles on a straight line

animations.alt_text: Two intersecting lines create four angles, with opposite pairs highlighted as equal.
animations.description: Demonstrates that vertically opposite angles are equal.

nc_references.statement: Recognise angles where they meet at a point, are on a straight line, or are vertically opposite, and find missing angles
Apply the properties of vertically opposite angles

animations.title: Proposition XV — vertically opposite angles

animations.alt_text: The three angles of a triangle are rearranged along a straight line to show a 180-degree total.
animations.description: Demonstrates that the interior angles of a triangle sum to 180 degrees.

nc_references.statement: Find unknown angles in triangles, quadrilaterals and regular polygons
Derive and use the sum of angles in a triangle and use it to deduce the angle sum in polygons

animations.title: Proposition XXXII — angle sum of a triangle
